summary: Completed testing of subscription_tier bug fix. All 11 backend tests passed (100%). Verified: 1) GET /api/tenants returns subscription_tier for all tenants, 2) GET /api/tenants/{code} returns subscription_tier correctly, 3) PUT /api/tenants/{code}/subscription allows super admin to change tier directly, 4) Subscription tier persists after update (no reset to basic), 5) Access control - non-super-admin cannot update subscription.

details: All tenants now return subscription_tier field
Super admin can update subscription tier
Custom max_users can be set with subscription update
Default max_users applied based on tier (basic=100, pro=500, enterprise=-1)
No reset to 'basic' on subsequent GET requests
max_users persists correctly with tier changes
Tenant admin (non-super-admin) correctly denied subscription update (403)

critical_code_review_comments: ✓ TenantResponse model now includes subscription_tier, max_users, primary_color, secondary_color fields
✓ list_tenants endpoint correctly populates all TenantResponse fields from database
✓ get_tenant endpoint correctly populates all TenantResponse fields from database
✓ New PUT /api/tenants/{code}/subscription endpoint properly validates tier values
✓ Access control correctly restricts subscription updates to super_admin only
seed_data_creation: None - used existing test data. Subscription tier changes during tests are restored to original values.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Subscription tier bug fix fully tested and working. Key findings: 1) TenantResponse model now properly includes subscription_tier, max_users, primary_color, secondary_color fields, 2) Both list_tenants and get_tenant endpoints correctly populate these fields, 3) New PUT /api/tenants/{code}/subscription endpoint works for super admins, 4) Murphy Shark tenant has 'pro' tier with max_users=500, 5) Access control properly restricts subscription updates to super_admin only.
